top_signals.title: From Shin Bet to Mossad, Netanyahu Reshapes Israeli Intelligence
top_signals.summary:
Editor’s Note: Rewind and Reconnoiter is one of our weekly members-only newsletters. To access the full archive of Rewind and Reconnoiter as well as our other members-only content, including podcasts, newsletters, and exclusive access to the War on the Rocks app, sign up using the link in the graphic below.In 2025, Ofek Riemer, Daniel Wajner, and Ehud Eiran wrote, “Populists vs. Spies in Israel and Beyond,” where they argued that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s relationship with Israel’s intelligence agencies could have grave consequences for Israel’s democracy. A year later, we asked them to revisit their arguments.Image: U.S. Department of State via Wikimedia

The post From Shin Bet to Mossad, Netanyahu Reshapes Israeli Intelligence appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of Manufacturers and Traders Trust Company
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of Manufacturers and Traders Trust Company
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Yield-bearing stablecoin slowdown ends 3-year run for crypto-native products
top_signals.summary: Yield-bearing stablecoin supply fell 15% in Q2 as sUSDe and sUSDS contracted, while Treasury-backed products including BUIDL, USYC and USDY continued to grow.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: ‘I think we’re nearing the bottom’: Bitwise CIO says Strategy’s STRC selloff is part of bitcoin’s end-of-cycle dynamics
top_signals.summary: Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan says Strategy's STRC selloff reflects the kind of "end-of-cycle" deleveraging that precedes a bitcoin market bottom.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.title: Analysis of US Jobs Report for June
top_signals.summary: Jobseekers during a job fair in Buffalo, New York. Photographer: Lauren Petracca/Bloomberg
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: JPMorgan Joins Gulf Banks on $7 Billion Loan for Syrian Projects
top_signals.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is joining Gulf lenders to help arrange about $7 billion of debt financing for a Qatari firm’s projects in Syria, marking one of the biggest foreign commitments to the country’s reconstruction since dictator Bashar al-Assad was ousted by rebel forces.

top_signals.title: Ford CEO wants level playing field with Toyota, GM imports as USMCA trade talks reopen
top_signals.summary: Ford reports it assembled more than 2 million vehicles in the U.S. last year – more than any other auto manufacturer, including 311,000 units for export.

top_signals.title: City watchdog suspends parts of £9bn motor finance scheme after industry backlash
top_signals.summary: The UK’s financial watchdog has suspended parts of its under-fire £9bn motor finance redress scheme amid fierce backlash from industry and consumer groups. In a statement on Thursday, the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) said the suspension will require firms to continue preparations for the scheme but avoid work that “may need to be repeated” if [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Sydney Expands Ferry Fleet with Two Newbuilds
top_signals.summary: Sydney has completed the rollout of its new Parramatta River Class ferry fleet, with the delivery of the final two vessels enabling the addition of 136 weekly services on the city's Parramatta River route.The final vessels, Norman Selfe and Jack Mundey…
